title: Cryphiacanthus australis
intro: Cryphiacanthus australis, also known as Guaco or Mexican Mint, is a shrubby perennial plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the Acanthaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cryphiacanthus australis include diterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cryphiacanthus australis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in various pharmaceutical formulations to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with infections.
